lede: A look at November 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Carl Sagan and Alessandro Del Piero, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Uttarakhand
events.desc: Uttarakhand officially becomes the 27th state of India, formed from thirteen districts of northwestern Uttar Pradesh.

events.title: Garry Kasparov
events.desc: Garry Kasparov, 22, of the Soviet Union, becomes the youngest World Chess Champion by beating fellow Soviet Anatoly Karpov.

events.title: Warsaw
events.desc: Warsaw is awarded the Virtuti Militari by the Polish government-in-exile.

events.title: Kristallnacht
events.desc: Kristallnacht occurs, instigated by the Nazis using the killing of German diplomat Ernst vom Rath by Herschel Grynszpan as justification.

events.title: Munich
events.desc: In Munich, police and government troops crush the Nazi Beer Hall Putsch.

events.title: Great Lakes Storm of 1913
events.desc: The Great Lakes Storm of 1913, the most destructive natural disaster ever to hit the lakes, reaches its greatest intensity after beginning two days earlier. The storm destroys 19 ships and kills more than 250 people.

events.title: Kentucky
events.desc: Kentucky marshals abduct abolitionist minister Calvin Fairbank from Jeffersonville, Indiana, and take him to Kentucky to stand trial for helping a slave escape.

events.title: Wrocław
events.desc: The Bohemian King Frederick I flees Prague to Vratislav one day after the defeat of his troops in the Battle of White Mountain.

births.name: Enrico De Nicola
births.line: Italian journalist, lawyer, and politician, 1st President of the Italian Republic

deaths.text: Joseph D. Early, American soldier and politician
Giovanni Leone, Italian lawyer and politician, 6th President of Italy
Charles de Gaulle, French general and politician, 18th President of France
Neville Chamberlain, English businessman and politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
Emperor Gaozong of Song, Emperor of China from 1127 to 1162

observances.text: Christian feast day: Dedication of the Archbasilica of St. John Lateran, Cathedral of the Pope (memorial feast day)
Christian feast day: Margery Kempe (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Theodore of Amasea (Roman Catholic Church)
